topics.title: Four mathematicians receive the 2026 Fields Medal
topics.summary: Yu Deng, John Pardon, Jacob Tsimerman and Hong Wang received the Fields Medal at the International Congress of Mathematicians. Their work spans number theory, geometry, analysis and mathematical physics, and Wang is the award’s third woman recipient.
topics.discussionPrompt: Math prizes celebrate ideas most people may never fully understand. What kind of achievement deserves more everyday recognition: deep theory, practical inventions, great teaching, or something else?

topics.title: Breath test tracks when the body burns fat
topics.summary: Researchers developed a handheld breath test that measures ketosis in real time by detecting molecules released during fat burning. Early results suggest it could help people monitor metabolic changes, though the device still needs testing in larger groups.
topics.discussionPrompt: Would instant feedback about how your body uses food motivate healthier habits, or would it turn eating and exercise into too much of a numbers game?

topics.title: The Odyssey offers clues to ancient science
topics.summary: A new examination of Homer’s Odyssey looks beyond its monsters and magic to find clues about ancient Greek botany, medicine, navigation and psychology. The epic may preserve practical knowledge alongside its famous storytelling.
topics.discussionPrompt: If future historians treated a favorite modern story as evidence about our science and daily life, what would they get surprisingly right—and hilariously wrong?

topics.title: A leaner Commonwealth Games opens in Glasgow
topics.summary: Glasgow is opening a streamlined Commonwealth Games built around 10 sports at four existing venues. The scaled-back event runs through August 2, testing whether a smaller format can keep a major international competition lively and affordable.
topics.discussionPrompt: Can a smaller sports festival feel more fun and focused than a giant one, or does cutting events take away the sense of occasion? Which sport would you protect first?
